title: Article Schema & Migration

story.i_want_to: store development knowledge articles (patterns, conventions, decisions, findings) in a structured, tenant-scoped schema
story.so_that: institutional knowledge is persisted in the database and available for search and retrieval across projects

rationale: Development teams and AI agents accumulate institutional knowledge during implementation loops -- patterns that work, decisions made, findings from reviews. Without a structured place to store this knowledge, it gets lost between sessions or buried in chat logs. The Article schema provides a first-class entity for capturing this knowledge with proper categorization (pattern, convention, decision, finding, reference), tenant scoping via RLS, and optional project association so knowledge can be either tenant-wide or project-specific.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Loopctl.Knowledge.Article schema exists with fields: id (binary_id PK), tenant_id (binary_id FK to tenants), project_id (binary_id FK to projects, nullable -- null means tenant-wide), title (string, max 500), body (text), category (Ecto.Enum: :pattern, :convention, :decision, :finding, :reference), status (Ecto.Enum: :draft, :published, :archived, :superseded), tags ({:array, :string}), source_type (string, nullable), source_id (binary_id, nullable), metadata (map/jsonb, default %{}), inserted_at, updated_at.
The schema uses `use Loopctl.Schema` and `tenant_field()` macro. The tenant_id field is NEVER included in cast -- it is always set programmatically.

A create_changeset/2 validates required fields: title, body, category. It casts title, body, category, status, tags, source_type, source_id, metadata, project_id. It validates title max length of 500 characters. It defaults status to :draft if not provided.
An update_changeset/2 allows partial updates to title, body, category, status, tags, metadata, project_id. It validates the same constraints as create_changeset where applicable.
Migration creates the articles table with all specified columns and correct types. Includes a GIN index on the tags column for efficient array containment queries.
Migration adds a composite index on (tenant_id, project_id, category) for efficient filtered listing queries.
Migration adds a unique index on (tenant_id, title) scoped to non-deleted articles, preventing duplicate titles within a tenant.
Migration enables RLS on the articles table via `import Loopctl.Repo.RlsHelpers` then `enable_rls(:articles)`. RLS policies enforce tenant isolation for SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE.
The source_type field accepts known string values: "review_finding", "manual", "agent", "session_log". Validation is advisory (not enforced as Ecto.Enum) to allow future extensibility.

The category Ecto.Enum values map to the five core knowledge types: :pattern (reusable implementation pattern), :convention (team/project convention), :decision (architectural or design decision), :finding (review finding or discovery), :reference (reference material or documentation link).

The status Ecto.Enum values support the full article lifecycle: :draft (work in progress), :published (visible and active), :archived (soft-deleted), :superseded (replaced by a newer article via ArticleLink).

test_cases.name: Create article with valid required fields
preconditions: Loopctl.Knowledge.Article module is compiled and available
steps: Build a changeset with valid title, body, category, and tenant_id set programmatically
expected_results: Changeset is valid with no errors

test_cases.name: Reject article missing required fields
preconditions: Loopctl.Knowledge.Article module is compiled and available
steps: Build a create_changeset with empty attrs (no title, body, or category)

test_cases.name: Reject title exceeding 500 characters
preconditions: Loopctl.Knowledge.Article module is compiled and available
steps: Build a create_changeset with a title of 501 characters
expected_results: Error on :title indicates max length exceeded

test_cases.name: Reject invalid category value
preconditions: Loopctl.Knowledge.Article module is compiled and available
steps: Build a create_changeset with category set to :invalid_category
expected_results: Error on :category indicates invalid enum value

test_cases.name: Insert article and enforce unique title per tenant
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: "Existing Article"}) as existing_article
steps: Attempt to insert a second article with the same title and tenant_id
expected_results: Insert fails with unique constraint error on (tenant_id, title)

test_cases.name: Allow same title across different tenants
steps: Insert an article with title "Shared Title" for tenant_b
expected_results: Insert succeeds -- title uniqueness is scoped to tenant_id

test_cases.name: Update changeset allows partial updates
preconditions: An existing article struct with all fields populated
steps: Build an update_changeset with only body changed
expected_results: Only body field is changed; other fields remain unchanged

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation via RLS prevents cross-tenant reads
expected_results: Query returns nil -- tenant_b cannot see tenant_a's articles via RLS

technical_notes: Schema MUST use `use Loopctl.Schema` which provides `@primary_key {:id, :binary_id, autogenerate: true}`, `@foreign_key_type :binary_id`, `@timestamps_opts [type: :utc_datetime_usec]`.
Use `tenant_field()` macro in the schema block to add the tenant_id field with correct FK and index.
Category enum: Ecto.Enum with values [:pattern, :convention, :decision, :finding, :reference]. Status enum: Ecto.Enum with values [:draft, :published, :archived, :superseded].
GIN index on tags: `create index(:articles, [:tags], using: :gin)` for efficient @> (array contains) and && (array overlap) queries.

Unique index on title per tenant: consider a partial index excluding archived/superseded status, or use a simple (tenant_id, title) unique index. The exact scoping ('non-deleted') should use a WHERE clause: `where: "status NOT IN ('archived')"` or similar.

The source_type and source_id fields enable provenance tracking -- linking an article back to the review finding, agent session, or manual entry that created it. These are intentionally loose (string + binary_id) rather than polymorphic FKs for extensibility.
Migration file: `priv/repo/migrations/TIMESTAMP_create_articles.exs`. Import `Loopctl.Repo.RlsHelpers` and call `enable_rls(:articles)` after table creation.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests.
